2605.04557.title: Efficient Geometry-Controlled High-Resolution Satellite Image Synthesis
2605.04557.paper_summary: High-resolution satellite images are often scarce and costly, especially for remote areas or infrequent events. This shortage hampers the development and testing of machine learning models for land-cover classification, change detection, and disaster monitoring. In this paper, we tackle the problem of geometry-controlled high-resolution satellite image synthesis by adding control over existing pre-trained diffusion models. We propose a simple yet efficient method for controlling the synthesis process by leveraging only skip connection features using windowed cross-attention modules. Several previously established control techniques are compared, indicating that our method achieves comparable performance while leading to a better alignment with the geometry control map. We also discuss the limitations in current evaluation approaches, amplifying the necessity of a consistent alignment assessment.
2605.04557.paper_summary_zh: 高解析度的衛星影像通常稀少且昂貴，特別是在偏遠地區或不常發生的事件中。這種短缺阻礙了機器學習模型在土地覆蓋分類、變化檢測和災害監測方面的發展和測試。在本文中，我們通過對現有的預訓練擴散模型進行控制，來解決幾何控制的高解析度衛星影像合成問題。我們提出了一種簡單但有效的方法，僅利用窗口交叉注意模塊中的跳躍連接特徵來控制合成過程。幾種先前建立的控制技術進行了比較，顯示我們的方法在性能上達到了可比性，同時在幾何控制圖的對齊上表現得更佳。我們還討論了當前評估方法的局限性，強調了一致性對齊評估的必要性。
2605.04557.author: Vlad Vasilescu et.al.
2605.04557.authors: Vlad Vasilescu, Daniela Faur, Teodor Costachioiu
